figures.name: fig76_su2k_fusion_tables

issues: Fusion channel text annotations (e.g., 'V_0+V_2') are very small and difficult to read at publication size, especially in the k=3 (Fibonacci) panel which has a 4x4 grid
Axis tick labels use Unicode subscripts (V_0, V_1, ...) but the text inside cells uses ASCII underscore notation (V_0+V_2) — inconsistent formatting between axes and cell text
Overall figure height (350px) is tight for three subplots; the k=3 panel is cramped relative to k=1

figures.notes: Fusion rules are correct. k=1 gives Z_2 (semion), k=2 gives Ising fusion (V_1 x V_1 = V_0 + V_2), k=3 gives Fibonacci-containing rules. The colorscale [white, steel blue] correctly matches project palette. Physics content is accurate. Main concern is readability at small size.

figures.name: fig77_su2k_quantum_dims

issues: At V_0, the text annotations '1.000' for all four bars (k=1,2,3,4) are heavily overlapping, rendering as '1.0001.0001.0001.000' — unreadable
The golden ratio annotation 'phi = (1+sqrt(5))/2' overlaps with the '1.732' text annotation for the k=4 bar at V_1
The y-axis label uses ASCII 'Quantum dimension d_j' rather than subscript notation 'd_j' — minor inconsistency with the title which uses HTML subscript

figures.notes: All numerical values are correct: k=1 gives (1, 1), k=2 gives (1, 1.414, 1), k=3 gives (1, 1.618, 1.618, 1), k=4 gives (1, 1.732, 2.000, 1.732, 1.000). The golden ratio annotation phi = (1+sqrt(5))/2 = 1.618 correctly labels V_1 at k=3. Colors use project palette (Rb87 blue, K39 berry, Na23 amber, dispersive green). The text overlap at V_0 and the annotation collision at V_1 are the primary rendering issues.

figures.name: fig78_su2k_s_matrix
figures.notes: S-matrix values are correct. k=1 (2x2): S = [[0.7071, 0.7071], [-0.7071, 0.7071]] up to row ordering. k=2 (3x3): diagonal (0.5, 0.0, 0.5), off-diag (0.7071, -0.7071) in correct positions. RdBu colorscale centered at zero is appropriate for real symmetric matrices with positive and negative entries. Cell annotations are legible. Only colorbar shown on right panel (avoids clutter). Clean layout with adequate spacing.

figures.name: fig79_hopf_chain

issues: Node labels are severely overlapping along the horizontal axis — the text strings (e.g., 'Onsager O (24 thms)', 'q-Onsager O_q (coideal)', 'U_q(sl_2) (6+21 thms)') collide and become illegible in the left portion of the diagram
Text is placed 'top center' for all nodes, but nodes at y=0 are spaced only 1 unit apart in x with long multi-line labels, causing horizontal text overlap
The node for U_q(sl_2-hat) at y=-1 is visually detached; its label is readable but the vertical offset makes the overall flow harder to follow
Edge lines are thin grey and somewhat difficult to see against the white background

The edge list [(0,1), (1,3), (2,4), (4,5), (5,6), (6,7), (2,3)] has no edge from q-Onsager (node 1) to U_q(sl_2) (node 2) — the chain appears disconnected between the Onsager algebra side and the quantum group side. Edge (1,3) connects q-Onsager to U_q(sl_2-hat) and edge (2,3) connects U_q(sl_2) to U_q(sl_2-hat), but the main horizontal chain skips from node 1 to node 4 via the detour through node 3. Verify this is the intended topology — it may be physically correct (q-Onsager embeds as coideal in affine, not finite, quantum group) but worth confirming
Node colors are varied (green, blue, berry, amber, purple) which is good for distinguishing modules, but there is no legend explaining what each color category represents
The diagram would benefit from directional arrows on edges rather than plain lines, to convey the 'chain' / flow semantics suggested by the title

figures.notes: The conceptual content is valuable — showing the mathematical chain from Onsager algebra through quantum groups to modular tensor category. The primary issue is rendering: horizontal node labels are too long and overlap. Recommend either (a) increasing figure width significantly, (b) using shorter labels and a separate legend, (c) alternating text positions (top/bottom), or (d) rotating to a vertical layout. The physics topology question about the missing (1,2) edge is minor and may be intentional.

figures.name: fig80_e8_cartan
figures.notes: E8 Cartan matrix is correct: 8x8 symmetric, diagonal = 2, off-diagonal entries are -1 exactly where the E8 Dynkin diagram has edges (alpha_1-alpha_3, alpha_2-alpha_4, alpha_3-alpha_4, alpha_4-alpha_5, alpha_5-alpha_6, alpha_6-alpha_7, alpha_7-alpha_8), all others zero. det = 1. The title states 'det=1, diagonal=2, sigma=8' which is correct (E8 is positive definite, signature = 8). The bottom annotation about Rokhlin is accurate: sigma(E8) = 8 which is 0 mod 8 but not 0 mod 16. Colorscale uses project blue (negative) / white (zero) / amber (positive) consistently. Cell annotations are legible. The y-axis is correctly reversed so alpha_1 appears at top.

summary: Three of five Phase 5c figures pass review (fig78 S-matrix, fig80 E8 Cartan, and fig76 fusion tables with minor text size concerns). Two figures have rendering issues requiring attention: fig77 (quantum dimensions bar chart) has severe text overlap at V_0 where four '1.000' annotations collide, and fig79 (Hopf chain diagram) has heavily overlapping node labels that make the diagram illegible. All figures are physically accurate with correct numerical values. No colorblind accessibility issues — the palette avoids red/green combinations.
recommendations: fig77: Fix text overlap at V_0 by either (a) reducing textfont size, (b) using textposition='auto' to let Plotly optimize placement, (c) suppressing annotations for trivial d=1.000 values, or (d) adding horizontal offset between grouped bar labels
fig77: Move the golden ratio annotation upward or to the right to avoid collision with the k=4 bar annotation at V_1
fig77: Use HTML subscript in y-axis label ('Quantum dimension d<sub>j</sub>') for consistency with title
fig79: Increase figure width from 1000 to 1400+ px, or shorten node labels to abbreviations (e.g., 'O (24)', 'O_q', 'U_q(sl_2) (27)', etc.) with a separate legend for full names
fig79: Alternate textposition between 'top center' and 'bottom center' for adjacent nodes to reduce horizontal text collision
fig79: Consider adding arrowheads to edges to convey directionality of the emergence chain
fig76: Consider increasing figure height from 350 to 400-450px to give the k=3 panel more room for its 4x4 grid annotations
